AB.description: Official at bats (does not include walks or sacrifices)

AVG.description: Hits divided by at bats – the most basic measure of batting success

ERA.description: Earned runs allowed per nine innings

HR/9.description: Home runs allowed per nine innings

OBP.description: How often a player reaches base per plate appearance

PA.description: Plate appearances – every completed trip to the plate

SLG.description: Total bases per at bat, reflecting power

WHIP.description: Walks and hits allowed per inning pitched

BABIP.description: Batting Average on Balls In Play. Measures batting average excluding home runs and strikeouts. Lower BABIP for pitchers = weaker contact allowed. League average is around .300

CSW.description: Called Strike + Whiff rate. Percentage of pitches that result in either a called strike or swing-and-miss. Higher is better for pitchers. Elite pitches have CSW > 0.35

Chase Rate.description: Percentage of pitches outside the strike zone that batters swing at. Also called O-Swing%. Higher chase rate = more deceptive pitch. Elite chase rates are > 0.30

Zone Rate.description: Percentage of pitches thrown in the strike zone. High zone rate pitches (FF, SI, CH) work in zone, low zone rate pitches (CU, SL, KC) work as chase pitches

O-Swing%.description: Outside Swing Percentage. Same as Chase Rate - percentage of pitches outside zone that batters swing at

Z-Contact%.description: Zone Contact Percentage. How often batters make contact on pitches in the strike zone. Lower is better for pitchers

Whiff Rate.description: Percentage of swings that result in misses. Higher is better for pitchers. Strikeout pitches typically have high whiff rates

xwOBA.description: Expected Weighted On-Base Average. Predicts offensive production based on quality of contact. Lower is better for pitchers

Spin Rate.description: Rotations Per Minute. How fast the ball spins. Higher spin generally means more movement on breaking balls

SP.description: Starting Pitcher. Pitches from the beginning of the game, typically goes 5-7 innings

RP.description: Relief Pitcher. Enters mid-game, typically pitches 1-3 innings

CL.description: Closer. Relief pitcher who enters in the 9th inning with a lead to finish the game

FF.description: Four-seam Fastball. Standard high-velocity fastball pitch

SI.description: Sinker (Two-seam Fastball). Fastball with downward movement that induces ground balls

FC.description: Cutter. Fastball with late horizontal break, generates weak contact

CU.description: Curveball. Breaking ball with 12-6 vertical drop. Highest CSW rate pitch

SL.description: Slider. Breaking ball with lateral movement. Good chase rate pitch

CH.description: Changeup. Off-speed pitch that mimics fastball arm action. Lowest BABIP - elite weak contact

FS.description: Splitter (Split-finger Fastball). Off-speed pitch with sharp downward break. Elite chase rate and CSW

ST.description: Sweeper. Slider variant with exaggerated horizontal break. Very low BABIP

KC.description: Knuckle Curve. Curveball gripped like a knuckleball. Balanced performance pitch

T1.description: Tier 1 pitch. Top-tier pitch effectiveness. In MMOLB: Slider, Knuckle Curve, Splitter

T2.description: Tier 2 pitch. Second-tier pitch effectiveness. In MMOLB: Changeup, Curveball, Cutter, Sweeper

T3.description: Tier 3 pitch. Third-tier pitch effectiveness. In MMOLB: Fastball, Sinker

HBP.description: Hit By Pitch. When a batter is struck by a pitched ball and awarded first base

RBI.description: Runs Batted In. Number of runs scored as a direct result of a batter's at-bat

WAR.description: Wins Above Replacement. Measures a player's total value compared to a replacement-level player

DH.description: Designated Hitter. Bats in place of the pitcher but does not play defense
